title: A Mirror, Not a Fate
subtitle: How to Read the Tarot
title_note: Working title — series is 'The Recursive Tarot' (Book I = the history volume, currently titled 'The Recursive Tarot'; Book III = the contributor volume). Final titles TBD by PlayfulProcess.
author: PlayfulProcess
creed: Read to know yourself, not to be told your fate. A card starts a conversation with what is already alive in you — so hold what you find loosely, author its meaning yourself, and keep the choice your own. Be honest about what you see and kind in what you make it mean. Relate to the card; never obey it.
note: Single source of truth: every chapter below IS the live course MDX in ../../course/. The website renders these; this manifest re-orders the same files into a book and exports to EPUB/PDF. Never fork chapter prose into this folder — edit the .mdx, rebuild.

front_matter.title: Before you cast — the one intention

front_matter.note: Series preface: the trilogy as one argument — game → mirror → contribution. ~800 words.

parts.title: Part I — Why read at all

chapters.title: How the Cards Can Work
chapters.note: The mechanism chapter — weaves in the deep-research synthesis.

chapters.title: Tarot & the Crack
chapters.note: The personal disappointment → turn that earns the mirror stance.

chapters.title: Intention Setting
chapters.note: Mirror vs. oracle; open vs. closed questions; the 'chill in the spine' as somatic salience. The lenses open from here.

parts.title: Part III — The lenses: an ecosystem of conscience
parts.note: Canonical voice order from viewers/voices.json. Each lens is presented faithfully in its own terms; any disagreement with a tradition lives here in the long form, never in the short intention. Living-teacher voices are titled by their school and say 'inspired by'.

chapters.title: Morality Is an Ecosystem
chapters.note: The methodological root: why the good is gardened, not legislated — and why autonomy must be respected.

chapters.title: Jung Reads the Tarot
chapters.note: Archetypes, active imagination, synchronicity-as-framing (not causal claim).

chapters.title: The Non-Dual Tantra Lens
chapters.note: Inspired by Christopher Wallis. School-titled, 'inspired by'.

chapters.title: The Post-Activism Lens
chapters.note: Inspired by Bayo Akomolafe. School-titled, 'inspired by'.

chapters.title: The Hospicing-Modernity Lens
chapters.note: Inspired by Vanessa Andreotti. School-titled, 'inspired by'.

parts.title: Part IV — A ritual to walk

chapters.title: Walking the Golden Dawn Path
chapters.note: Pathworking as the practitioner ritual — gate, not fate. Candidate for an added step-by-step pathworking appendix (the 'ritual for Golden Dawn stuff').

parts.title: Part V — The long human practice

chapters.title: How Humans Have Always Cast Lots
chapters.note: Bridge chapter from the research: protocol / who / purpose / theology-of-randomness across Ifá, Hebrew lots & Urim/Thummim, I Ching, Delphi & Roman sortes/augury/haruspicy, runes, oracle bones, Tibetan Mo. Two recurring purposes (decision under uncertainty; impartial arbitration) and two theologies of randomness (voice of the more-than-human; guaranteed fairness). Waves at Book I's history.

back_matter.note: Author identity privacy: PlayfulProcess only, never a real name.

back_matter.text: CC-BY-SA-4.0 — the decks, the spreads, and these courses are public, open data in one GitHub repo.

epub.note: MDX → strip frontmatter + rewrite course-viewer links to internal anchors → pandoc → EPUB.

pdf.note: EPUB → KDP-ready PDF, mirroring the book-repo epub-build pipeline.

status.summary: All 11 chapters written. Front/back connective tissue (series preface, about page) remains. All 4 lens essays (jung / non-dual-tantra / post-activism / hospicing-modernity) + divination-traditions wired into the global Courses nav (site-header.js).
